_note: Guideline-authoring craft criteria, single SoT. judge=A `gdl.no-deliberation-narrative` is measured by `evaluate_deliberation_narrative` in checks.py — the caller (tools/_lint/meta_schema.py voice pass) injects the target text; the count of matches must be <= 0. judge=M criteria keep definition + pass_condition and are applied in the blind review pass (SKILL.md 'Blind Review Protocol'). Authoring prose with examples is in SKILL.md.

gdl.no-deliberation-narrative.name: No deliberation narrative
gdl.no-deliberation-narrative.source: Recitals vs operative provisions — EU legislative drafting guide
gdl.no-deliberation-narrative.note: Decision option names, reinforcement counters, introduction timestamps, changelog sections, and recurrence-prevention narratives are recital material — history belongs in log.md, the body states only current policy.

gdl.rule-not-reason.name: Rule, not reason
gdl.rule-not-reason.pass_condition: Each rule states what to do and when; any rationale is at most one line naming the trigger condition, never a narrative of how the rule was decided.

gdl.reason-not-pointer.name: Local obligation, pointer for depth
gdl.reason-not-pointer.pass_condition: A rule states its obligation locally in one sentence; a cross-reference supplies depth but never replaces the obligation ('see X' alone is not a rule).

gdl.exception-reasoned.name: Exceptions carry their condition
gdl.exception-reasoned.pass_condition: Every exception names the condition that licenses it; a bare 'except X' without a licensing condition fails.

gdl.universal-claim-verified.name: Universal claims verified
gdl.universal-claim-verified.pass_condition: Universal quantifiers about system behavior ('always', 'never', 'every') appear only when verified against the current code/corpus or qualified ('as measured', 'currently').

gdl.sot-link-not-restate.name: Link the SoT, do not restate it
gdl.sot-link-not-restate.pass_condition: No paragraph re-narrates a policy already stated by a table row or another SoT; the duplicate is replaced with a cross-reference.

gdl.self-contained.name: Self-contained meaning
gdl.self-contained.pass_condition: A reader can state what the rule obliges without opening another document, prior session context, or history.

gdl.executable.name: Executable by a fresh reader
gdl.executable.pass_condition: A Claude with no prior knowledge can perform the procedure from the text alone — inputs, order, and completion criteria are all present (the Claude-reproducibility principle).

gdl.normative-restraint.name: One force tier per rule
gdl.normative-restraint.pass_condition: Each rule sits at exactly one MUST/SHOULD/MAY tier with tier-consistent wording; hedged stacks ('should generally always') fail.

gdl.prune-or-enforce.name: Prune or enforce
gdl.prune-or-enforce.pass_condition: Every rule has an enforcement surface (lint, hook, review lens) or a deletion rationale; rules kept 'as documentation' fail.

gdl.absorb-before-adding.name: Absorb before adding
gdl.absorb-before-adding.pass_condition: A new section, table, or file exists only where absorption into an existing pattern was checked and does not fit (SKILL.md 'Bloat Control' 5-step + red flags).
